hotspots.title: Trump administration’s Iran war costs and strategies
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration faces intense scrutiny over its military engagement with Iran, including troop withdrawals from Germany, rising fuel prices, and a looming 60-day War Powers deadline. Congressional hearings and public protests highlight the financial and human costs of the conflict.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its high-stakes geopolitical implications, economic impact, and the moral outrage over prolonged military action without clear congressional approval.

hotspots.title: Magyar Péter’s nepotism and government formation
hotspots.blurb: Hungary’s incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar faces backlash for appointing his brother-in-law as justice minister, alongside other controversial cabinet picks. The move has sparked accusations of nepotism and undermined public trust in his anti-corruption platform.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the blatant nepotism, the erosion of public trust in governance, and the moral panic over the new government’s commitment to transparency.

hotspots.title: Rassemblement National’s Mâcon rally and 2027 ambitions
hotspots.blurb: France’s far-right Rassemblement National (RN) held its annual May Day rally in Mâcon, with Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella addressing supporters. The event underscored internal divisions, economic policy debates, and the party’s preparations for the 2027 presidential election.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the high-stakes leadership dynamics, the moral charge of far-right rhetoric, and the looming judicial decision that could bar Le Pen from running in 2027.

hotspots.title: Vox’s ‘national priority’ and regional pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s far-right Vox party is campaigning on its ‘national priority’ platform ahead of Andalusia’s regional elections, while negotiating government pacts with the conservative PP in Castilla y León and Aragón. The party’s anti-immigration stance and clashes with the Catholic Church have intensified political tensions.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the charged rhetoric around immigration, the moral panic over far-right influence in regional governments, and the polarized contention between Vox and mainstream parties.

hotspots.title: Reform UK’s Nigel Farage and electoral surge
hotspots.blurb: Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, is making significant gains in local elections, with a focus on anti-immigration policies and criticism of mainstream parties. The party’s rise has been fueled by a £5 million donation from a crypto billionaire and internal controversies over candidate selections.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to the high-profile leadership of Nigel Farage, the moral panic over far-right electoral success, and the polarized contention with mainstream parties over immigration and economic policies.
